content.content:
# Card View Documentation Analysis

## Concrete Claims Found

**View Mode Switchers:**
The documentation mentions toggling between views: "A card that opens an expandable panel on the right. A card view that toggles into a table or list view."

**Grid Layouts:**
The pattern uses a gallery grid approach with responsive behavior. Cards "will size down appropriately based on the built-in CSS responsiveness behaviors" and "follow the grid layout, expanding and compressing at certain breakpoints."

**Information Hierarchy:**
Cards present "data set summaries, allowing users to drill down into any card to see more detailed content." The design emphasizes visual identification: "Display items in a data set that are best identified by a graphic or other visual representation."

**Card Components:**
Required/optional elements include:
- Card body or footer for single inline actions
- Card header with top-right kebab for multiple actions
- Checkboxes in top-right corner for global actions
- Optional expandable sections with "expand all/collapse all" controls

**Grid Spacing & Backgrounds:**
Two background options exist based on use case—white backgrounds when views can change, gray backgrounds when cards are the sole content display. The documentation notes: "Use the default border treatment (cards with drop shadows) for cards when placed on a gray background only. Flat cards should be used when placing cards on a white background."

**Mobile Responsiveness:**
At smallest sizes, cards "take up almost the entire width of the screen."

**No UX Research Findings:** The documentation contains no explicit research data or studies.

**No Masonry/Bento/Featured Cards:** These layouts are not mentioned in the provided content.
